title: terebinthus vulgaris
intro: Terebinthus vulgaris, also known as the common turpentine tree or the prickly ash, is a deciduous shrub native to the Mediterranean region. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and expectorant properties. The health benefits of Terebinthus vulgaris include relief from respiratory issues such as coughs and colds, as well as reducing inflammation and pain. The herbal preparations of Terebinthus vulgaris include essential oils, tinctures, and infusions, which can be used to treat various ailments, including skin conditions and digestive issues.
